meta.title: Zimbra Mail Notifier — Chrome & Firefox Extension
meta.description: Zimbra Mail Notifier — Chrome and Firefox extension to monitor your Zimbra mailbox: unread messages, calendar, tasks and notifications.

donate.msg: This extension notifies you when new messages arrive in your Zimbra mailbox.

hero.title: Zimbra Mail Notifier
hero.subtitle: Monitor your Zimbra account and get notified of unread messages. View upcoming appointments and track ongoing tasks — all from your browser toolbar.

features.title: Features
features.intro: Browser extension written in TypeScript to monitor one or more Zimbra accounts in real time.
features.unreadDesc: Badge counter on the toolbar icon, list of recent emails with regex filter and "inbox only" option.
features.calendarDesc: Upcoming appointments with configurable reminders (delay, repeats) and desktop notifications.
features.tasksDesc: Track ongoing tasks sorted by priority (high, normal, low).
features.draftsDesc: Display unsent draft messages directly in the popup.
features.notifDesc: Desktop alerts and customizable sounds (ding, drain, ping, heal or custom audio file) with volume control.
features.accountsDesc: Multiple accounts, 2FA authentication, trusted device, AES-encrypted passwords and optional auth cookie sync.

install.title: Installation
install.intro: Zimbra Mail Notifier is an extension for Chrome and Firefox browsers.
install.step1: Click the button for your browser above.
install.step2: On the store page, click Add to Chrome or Add to Firefox.
install.step3: Confirm the installation when your browser asks.
install.step4: The Zimbra Mail Notifier icon appears in the toolbar. Click it to configure your first account.
install.devCallout: For developers: clone the GitHub repository, run npm install && npm run build, then load the dist/ folder via your browser's developer mode (chrome://extensions or about:debugging).
install.uninstallDesc: To remove the extension, open your browser's extension manager, find "Zimbra Mail Notifier" and click Uninstall.

manual.title: User manual
manual.step1: Click the extension icon in the toolbar, then the gear icon (Preferences).
manual.step2: In the Accounts tab, click Add new account.
manual.step3: Enter the Zimbra server URL (e.g. https://mail.example.com) and the web interface URL if different.
manual.step4: Enter your username and password. Check Save password for automatic login.
manual.step5: Click Connect to test the configuration. For 2FA, follow the on-screen instructions.
manual.optGeneralDesc: Auto-connect on startup, cookie sync to open the web interface already authenticated, popup color and width.
manual.optMailDesc: Enable unread management, max displayed count, regex filter, inbox only, notifications and sounds.
manual.optCalendarDesc: Display period (weeks), appointment count, reminders with delay and repeats, sound notifications.
manual.optTasksDesc: Priority filtering, max tasks and drafts displayed in the popup.
manual.tipCallout: Tip: use the Refresh (↻) button in the popup to force an immediate check of your accounts.

versions.title: Version history
versions.intro: Release timeline generated from the project's Git history.

items.desc: Option to read emails only from the main folder (inbox).
New design, TypeScript migration, Webpack build (replaces Maven). Native Firefox support. Draft display.
Fix web interface URL field enable/disable state.
Fix unread messages on Zimbra 8.8.9 without sortBy.
jQuery v3.2.1 update, cleanup of unnecessary files.
Customizable popup color and width, notification sound selection.
Task sorting by priority, multi-account management.
Free/Alice ADSL account support, HTTPS fix.
First public release. Notifications, calendar, tasks, AES password encryption, DE/ES/IT/PT/TR locales.

compat.title: Compatibility
compat.chromeDesc: Manifest V3 — available on the Chrome Web Store.
compat.firefoxDesc: Native support since v4.0 — available on Firefox Add-ons.
compat.supported1: All sites using the standard Zimbra login interface
compat.unsupported1: Pre-authentication portals (except Free and Alice ADSL)
compat.unsupported2: Safari and Opera (historical support, not maintained in v4)
compat.langsDesc: The interface is available in 15 languages: English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Brazilian Portuguese, Dutch, Polish, Russian, Japanese, Korean, Simplified Chinese, Serbian and Turkish.

help.title: Help & contact
help.intro: For any question, suggestion or bug report:
help.connFailDesc: Check the server URL (with https://), your credentials and that your Zimbra server is reachable from your network.
help.noNotifDesc: Make sure notifications are enabled in the extension options and allowed in your browser / system settings.
help.wrongCountDesc: Enable the "inbox only" option if you want to exclude subfolders from the count.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This document explains what information Zimbra Mail Notifier collects and how it is used.
privacy.collected1: Login credentials — username and password entered in the extension settings page.
privacy.collected2: This data is stored locally in your browser storage (AES encryption for passwords).
privacy.usage1: Credentials are sent to the Zimbra server only during automatic login.
privacy.usage2: No data is sent to third-party servers.
privacy.usage3: No usage statistics are collected (Google Analytics removed since v1.0.0).
privacy.permissionsCallout: Required permissions: notifications, cookies, local storage, network access to your configured Zimbra server URLs.

about.title: About
about.name: Zimbra Mail Notifier
about.desc: This extension notifies you when new messages arrive in your Zimbra mailbox.
about.legal: This extension is not affiliated or endorsed by VMware.
